item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot! I think you could do to use the word "believed" a little less though

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
None of the images could be reliably sourced and are assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, they have since been removed.

The second image of the Venera project was traced back to NASA on Commons; however, Wikimedia Commons ruled that it is not public domain and was taken down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made a change to the first paragraph of the essay:

The mission would jointly serve to investigate the anomaly, as well as jointly testing the limits of our new technology.

Removed the second "jointly".

Just pointing it out in case anyone wants to revert. Because it toes the line between a grammar fix and a style choice, especially in what is supposed to be a less formal section of a well-established skip.

Also, that unfixable typo in the last picture is going to give me a facial tic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The so-called essay (which should really be termed an informal report) reads as if the doctor wrote it on their lunch break. The tone is off, it feels awkward (three successive sentences beginning with although??) and it really adds nothing much to the wider narrative. It's a shame because this is an idea which definitely lends itself well to the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's no rule that says you can't put images there (or anywhere in a document). Given that this is both relatively old and very successful, I would think that we could assume that this was intentional, or it would have been changed by now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image is under the object class instead of above the item number, can I fix that?

EDIT: Somehow, my forum posts always end up create new pages on the forum page XD

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Milo359, you're replying to an almost three-year old post. Chances are, it'd be a lot faster to just PM a mod.

Anyway, spoiler has been collapsed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mod should put this in a spoiler (collapsible).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't matter, it's been fixed. Thanks for the assistance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually did upload the image to the page sorry should have been more specific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just click Files underneath the page and follow the instructions. I'll handle the code. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT I found the original image i won"t try adding it because i"m worried i might fuck up the formatting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The author hotlinked, so when the image disappeared from its source site, the article lost it too. I've commented out the image code for now. It can be uncommented again when the author's found the image or a replacement, and uploaded it to the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the images appears to be dead. Does anyone have a backup copy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the basic idea a lot, but I feel it's presented very very awkwardly. I believe the payoff of the whole article should be the theories for the origins of the SCP, not the exploration. I also think that the exploration should not be presented in that essay format, which seems awkward and out of tone.

It would require a substantial re-write, but to get my upvote (which the idea surely deserves), it would have to start with a description of what the various things are, then an transcript of the exploration attempt written in a more military style (e.g. 0637: Probe Beta crash lands, and Probe Delta is redirected to the source of the radio waves), and end with something which hints at origins and allows us to work them out ourselves, rather than explicitly says 'here's what it is'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where'd you get the photos?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um … "SCP INDENTIFIED"?

Is the grey goo in an overextended margin?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No doubt that the water contributed to the runaway greenhouse effect that's made it a horrifying place now, but I *think* the chances of there being intelligent life a billion years ago when Venus may have had water is damn near zero :( it took Earth this long to get here, what could Venus have had in terms of life? Certainly no species waging war with bioweapons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a huge fan of the nanobot-superweapon concept, but it's executed very well - top marks for tying it into the Soviet Venera programme, which I hadn't heard about before. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Venus was once a much nicer place than it is now according to scientists at the ESA working on the Venus Express probe http://www.esa.int/esaSC/SEMQ9OLZLAG_index_0.html (Under Foundation supervision I would assume ;) )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'MMA BLASTIN OFF
BABY I'M VENUS BOUND
(here I come Venus)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because the log was translated by magic and kinda broke the feel something fierce

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why was the pattern screamer reference removed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Venusian Gray Goo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly the question you should leave people asking, in my opinion. Let people draw their own conclusions. The implications of Venus' atmosphere being completely infested with nanobots are far more interesting than a journal speculating that it must have been a doomsday thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just feel like if I remove the theorising then it won't make much sense. I just feel people will think 'what's the point of putting flesh-eating nanobots in the atmosphere when nothing lives there?'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like that most of the article is speculation on what it could be, rather than what it is. There's too much theorizing going on in comparison to actual description or analysis. It's also pretty silly that instead of an actual log of events, formally written up for the purpose of recording the events, they're using an essay some doctor just happened to write up.

Overall, I'm not a fan of the Ancient Alien doomsday nanobot concept, and I think the execution is lacking. This could probably get turned into something pretty excellent, and I really want to like it, but as it is I'm downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Pattern Screamers are a species of sentient, loud, plaid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Pattern Screamer is Yog Sothoth's second cousin.
Pattern Screamers live in the spaces between the pixels on your screen.
You can wear a Pattern Screamer as a hat, but it will scream at the movies and get you kicked out of the theater.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very ambitious, an SCP who's range of influence is the surface of a known planet. I was hesitate, but it turned out really well. Upvoted with pleasure, looking forward to more from you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I AM THE VERY MODEL OF AN ELDRITCH ABOMINATION.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to imagine a "Patter Screamer" is an ancient cosmic entity of destruction that bellows Gilbert and Sullivan at its victims. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Pattern-Screamer line was in the now-removed log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "SCP-1964-A spontaneously began to grow a mustache and came into possession of two maracas. A possible connection to Mexican SCP-173 is being investigated."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Has the Pattern Screamer bit been excised, or am I just missing it?

Anyway, I think the third paragraph of the description ought to be done away with. It's what the reader should be taking away from the article on their own, with the final paragraph putting them on the right track in case they don't jump to the conclusion automatically.

There's something about this that just demands a cheesy 60's sci-fi flick title. "Attack of the Killer Fleshbots from Venus!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of Venus being a wasteland due to some crazy shit is far-fetched but it was a good read and i love to see some more work about Patter Screamers. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i would find it more suspicious, but a lot of work seem to have went into this article, more so than could have been done in the few hours between broadcast and posting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did my best to phrase it in a way that only those who had already seen it would know what I was talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my. That'd be quite something. I'd be glad to help out with it, though I don't really get a huge amount of free time. I'll do what I can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of people felt the main character, Hamilton, was too similar to a character in a Warhammer novel. So, I deleted it. Anyway, I know this is a lot to ask, but maybe you'd like to collaborate on the Pattern Scremer SCP? I could send you my first draft in a few days, if you're up for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes yes yes. I'd love it, especially if you could take it in a different direction to 1050. Also, what happened to The Final Days of SCP-1840-J? I really enjoyed that, and now it seems to be deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like an octopus wearing a sombrero.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too much suspension of disbelief here for me to enjoy it, with regards to the Venusian ecosystem). And the majority of the article still feels like unwarranted speculation (and fanciful speculation at that) rather than anything the Foundation can actually prove.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you want to write it, write it.

But if it is a Reaper-type thing like Djoric suggested, keep in mind that we've already got one of those.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, would you guys be interested in me actually writing the Pattern Screamer SCP? I do have an idea for what they are…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great story…but the idea of Venus's hell-like conditions being the result of a doomsday weapon is very far-fetched when you take into account its proximity to the Sun :( I just can't buy into that knowing life couldn't have existed there in the first place.

Really great story though! If that one plot-hole was filled, I'd happily up-vote, but for now, I'll stand by the sidelines (although with all those votes, not like it matters :D)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't mind seeing that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-001: Talking Animals", here we come. :)

I will kill you so hard Smapti I swear to God

(the sad thing is you could probably pull it off you ridiculous bastard)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "SCP-001: Talking Animals", here we come. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So since the log has proven unpopular, I have deleted it. I have also tried to patch up some of the plot holes that have been identified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "Pattern Screamer" is more like an Easter Egg than a story arc at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like to see that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of giving every multi-cannon on the new site (which would benefit from such) its own SCP-001 would be an interesting one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really want to like this. I am totally down for a Foundation space program finding crazy shit outside of Earth.

I'm not sure about the overall execution here though. The pictures are fantastic, but crazy space nano re-assemblers are pretty old hat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would be more than a little okay with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suspect an SCP-001 proposal, 'Pattern Screamers', will emerge from all of this.

Hopefully a contest?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to like this, but there's too many holes. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) Where did the nanite-type things get organic matter? The probes wouldn't bring any (except for the latex coating on the wires), and all organic matter native to the planet had been cannibalized millions of years ago.

2) If the SCP can cannibalize what we humans would recognize as organic matter, and produce stuff we'd recognize as biological structures from that matter, then life on Venus must have had at least a few similarities to Earth life, namely that it would require water. So the doomsday device would not just have to account for the huge amounts of greenhouse gases, but also the lack of water.

3) How'd the Foundation manage to translate a single word of the transmission? If, as others have implied, the language has been encountered in other SCPs, at least needs to be linked from this article; preferably, the article should stand on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, is there anything in the articles that would lead us to believe that the two use different languages? The last addendum of the space hearts suggests that whatever created them was originally from this neck of the woods (possibly our progenitors?), and refer to similar enough subject matter that I'm tempted to peg this as the same civilization and language. Hell, my headcanon wants to say that the monolith that with the engraved warnings from Stalin and other historical leaders about "the destroyers" could be linked as well…

EDIT: the cyclical take on the name Pattern Screamers is an interesting one. Personally I ended up reading it as something closer to "Anti-spirals" in the Gurren Lagaan sense…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAAH!
There is no escape.
